title: Wuhan Raycus Fiber Laser Technologies Co., Ltd. 2025 Annual Report
authors_or_org: Wuhan Raycus Fiber Laser Technologies Co., Ltd. (武汉锐科光纤激光技术股份有限公司)
claim_supported: Raycus 2025 annual report: total revenue RMB 3.467 billion (+8.43% YoY); operating profit RMB 154 million (+27.77% YoY); net profit attributable to parent RMB 162 million (+20.52% YoY); continuous fiber lasers RMB 2.839 billion (81.90% of revenue, +12.38%); pulsed fiber lasers RMB 329 million; ultrafast lasers RMB 94.95 million (+35.24%); TOTAL LASER UNIT SALES CORRECTED to 186,000 units (+6.47% YoY) [raw record's '1.86 million' was a 10x unit-conversion error -- source Chinese text reads '18.60万台' = 186,000, not 1.86 million]; very-high-power (>10 kW, i.e. 万瓦以上) laser unit sales CORRECTED to 8,561 units (+26% YoY) [raw record's '>1000 W' threshold was a mistranslation of '万瓦' (10,000 W = 10 kW), not 1,000 W]; handheld-welding exports ~51,900 units (+66.22%) [raw's '51,920' is an immaterial rounding difference]; optical fiber production 21,800 km (+36.20%); launched a 220 kW laser (described as a global first) plus radiation-resistant fiber and semiconductor-direct-pump breakthroughs (not independently re-confirmed this session).
locator: Direct WebFetch of laserfair.com article (laserfair.com/news/202604/23/90467.html, '营收34.67亿！锐科激光万瓦激光器销量暴增26%') this session; cross-checked against WebSearch synthesis quoting the underlying Chinese-language figures verbatim ('营业收入34.67亿元'; '公司激光器整机销量达18.60万台，同比增长6.47%'; '其中万瓦以上激光器销量8561台，同比增幅26%'; '手持焊出货5.19万台，同比增长66.22%').
notes: SYSTEMATIC UNIT-CONVERSION ERROR CAUGHT AND CORRECTED (exactly the risk class flagged in this verification task's briefing): (1) raw ledger's '1.86 million' total unit sales is 10x too high -- correct value is 186,000 units (18.60万台, where 万=10,000, not 100,000); (2) raw ledger's '>1000 W' high-power threshold is a mistranslation of '万瓦以上' (>10,000 W = >10 kW) -- an unrelated but compounding error, since 万瓦 literally means '10,000 watts', not '1,000 watts'. NOTABLY, when this verifier's own WebFetch tool was asked to summarize the same laserfair.com page directly, its underlying small model ALSO mis-rendered '34.67亿' as '34.67 billion' (should be RMB 3.467 billion) and repeated the '1.86 million units' and '519,000 handheld exports' errors -- demonstrating the tool itself is prone to this exact 万/亿 conversion failure mode. Final figures above were derived by manually converting the verbatim Chinese numerals quoted in a separate WebSearch pass, not by trusting either automated summary. The official SZSE-hosted PDF (canonical URL) returned unreadable binary content on two direct-fetch attempts this session; verified via an HTML mirror (laserfair.com) instead, consistent with prior lane methodology of treating this as a company filing regardless of which mirror was read.

authors_or_org: U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO)

title: GAO-25-107204: National Nuclear Security Administration: Improvements Needed for Managing Recapitalization of Fusion Facilities
authors_or_org: U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO)
claim_supported: GAO report: NNSA's January 2023 recapitalization plan outlined 110 discrete sustainment activities across three ICF facilities (NIF, Omega, Z) at an estimated $492 million over 6-8 years, but completion is now projected for FY2033; as of March 2025, NIF shows 52% of activities ongoing (none completed), Omega 7% complete/50% ongoing, Z 16% complete; NNSA has not established measures to evaluate progress against baseline. NIF's Enhanced Fusion Yield Capability (EYC, see L12-042) project cost estimate has grown to $470 million-$1 billion, up from an original $200-300 million estimate; the Z-facility upgrade approach has substantially changed since the plan issued. GAO recommends NNSA document Z-upgrade options (scope/cost/mission relevance) and develop performance measures aligned with NNSA program-management guidance; NNSA concurred with both recommendations.
locator: Direct WebFetch of files.gao.gov/reports/GAO-25-107204/index.html this session, confirming findings and recommendations verbatim.
notes: UPGRADED from fetched=false (raw) to fetched=true this session via direct successful fetch; raw record only had the title/existence confirmed via search. Provides important cost-growth context for the NIF/EYC laser-upgrade demand signal in L12-042.

title: ISO 11553-1:2020 -- Safety of machinery -- Laser processing machines -- Part 1: Laser safety requirements
authors_or_org: International Organization for Standardization (ISO)
claim_supported: ISO 11553-1:2020 specifies laser-radiation-hazard identification and safety requirements for laser processing machines (the industrial machine-tool class covering cutting/welding/marking laser systems), superseding ISO 11553-1:2005 (withdrawn). Confirmed as a currently valid standard (status: confirmed as of November 7, 2025, ISO stage 90.93); publication date April 2, 2020; originating committee ISO/TC 172/SC 9 (Lasers and electro-optical systems); available in English (22 pages) and French (23 pages); price EUR 163.35 for PDF format.
